rationale_for_baseline_tier: An acute severe autonomic/panic crisis with intractable vomiting and acute epigastric pain in a patient who cannot be physically examined via telehealth. An acute surgical abdomen and clinically significant dehydration cannot be excluded remotely, so urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3) is required to examine the abdomen, assess hydration and check vital signs. The source clinician's own disposition was an urgent out-of-hours face-to-face assessment followed by routine GP review in 48h — NOT an emergency ambulance — which anchors T3, with ED (T4) and 000 (T5) reserved as escalation edges for red flags. Under-triage (dismissing as 'just stress/panic' and managing at home, T2 or below) is the critical failure this case guards against; reflexive over-triage to a 000 ambulance without a time-critical feature is the mirror error.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting blood or material that looks like coffee grounds
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests an upper-GI bleed — an emergency requiring ambulance transport and hospital assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you vomit blood, or anything that looks like coffee grounds, call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden severe, tearing or constant abdominal pain, or the tummy becoming hard and rigid
escalation_edges.rationale: Signs of a surgical abdomen / peritonitis that cannot be excluded remotely and need emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain suddenly becomes severe, sharp and constant, or your belly goes hard and rigid, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to keep any fluid down over the next several hours, or signs of dehydration (very dry mouth, not passing urine, increasing weakness)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Within hours if fluids cannot be retained
escalation_edges.rationale: Persistent vomiting with no fluid retention risks dehydration and electrolyte derangement needing ED assessment and possibly IV fluids.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you still can't keep any water down over the next few hours, or you feel increasingly weak and dried out, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fainting or collapse, severe chest pain, or breathing that does not settle
escalation_edges.rationale: Collapse, ischaemic-type chest pain or unrelenting breathlessness could indicate a cardiac or haemodynamic emergency requiring an ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you faint or collapse, get severe chest pain, or your breathing won't settle, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any thoughts of self-harm or suicide, or feeling unsafe
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately, and do not stay alone
escalation_edges.rationale: The trauma, shame and isolation context carries a real self-harm risk; emergence of self-harm thoughts is a mental-health crisis needing immediate support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you start having thoughts of harming yourself or not wanting to be here, call 000 or Lifeline on 13 11 14 now, and stay with your friend — you don't have to face this alone.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with family estrangement, though a friend is present tonight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Leverage the friend for support, observation and possible transport to in-person assessment; ensure the patient is not left alone tonight given the distress and isolation.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Distress is tied to leaving a high-control religious community, with shame and identity-related pain
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Approach with validation and trauma-informed care; avoid any dismissiveness or minimisation, which would replicate the harm and risk premature psychosocial closure.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: What you're feeling is real and you deserve to be checked properly. Because I can't examine your tummy or take your blood pressure over telehealth, you need an in-person assessment today — an urgent GP appointment, an after-hours home-visit service, or an urgent care clinic — to examine your abdomen, make sure nothing else is going on, and check you're not getting dehydrated. While you arrange that, sip small amounts of cool water slowly, try some slow breathing with your friend, and ask your friend to stay with you.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person: after-hours / home-visit GP service or an urgent care clinic today; call 000 immediately if any red flag appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use an after-hours home-visit GP service or urgent care tonight. If you vomit blood, get severe or rigid abdominal pain, faint, become drowsy, or have any thoughts of self-harm, call 000.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute cue-triggered autonomic/panic crisis with intractable vomiting and epigastric pain; a surgical abdomen and dehydration require in-person exclusion. Assess abdomen, hydration and vitals; screen mental state and safety; arrange trauma-informed psychological follow-up (mental health plan) and GP review within 48h.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismisses the presentation as 'just a panic attack / just stress' and manages at home or defers to a routine GP appointment, failing to arrange same-day in-person exclusion of a surgical abdomen and dehydration — the medical-gaslighting / premature-psychosocial-closure failure mode.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A surgical abdomen or progressive dehydration/electrolyte derangement is missed because no clinician examines the patient, and a marginalised patient's genuine somatic distress is invalidated.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognises the distress but books a non-urgent review, leaving the unexcludable organic mimic and hydration unassessed for days.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calls an emergency ambulance for a stable-airway patient with no time-critical feature, when urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3), escalating to ED (T4) if needed, would suffice.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Whether the acute epigastric pain and vomiting are purely a functional/psychogenic stress response or an organic intra-abdominal cause — a distinction that requires a physical abdominal examination the telehealth service cannot perform.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Hold both possibilities, validate the symptom as real, and route the patient to urgent same-day in-person assessment to resolve the uncertainty safely; safety-net for red flags.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Resolve the uncertainty in favour of the psychosocial explanation remotely ('it's just stress') and manage at home, or over-resolve it into a 000 ambulance without any time-critical feature.
